compiled_state.title: As a sprint orchestrator, I can merge a worktree branch in a single harvest-worktree.sh invocation
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Create harvest-worktree.sh that orchestrates the full worktree-to-session merge: verify worktree gates, merge with --no-commit, auto-resolve .test-index, attest gate status, and commit — all in one invocation.
**Why**: The manual merge workflow requires 6-8 tool calls per worktree merge. This script reduces it to 1 call while preserving gate integrity via the attestation mode from Story 2.
**Scope**:
- IN: harvest-worktree.sh accepting <worktree-branch> and <worktree-path> arguments; gate verification (read worktree artifacts dir, check test-gate-status and review-status); git merge --no-commit; conflict detection (abort on non-.test-index conflicts); call record-test-status.sh --attest and record-review.sh --attest; git commit; MERGE_HEAD cleanup on failure via trap; exit codes (0=success, 1=conflict, 2=gate-verification-failed); register the script in the dso shim
- OUT: Changes to pre-commit hooks; changes to compute-diff-hash.sh; the attestation mechanism itself (Story 2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running harvest-worktree.sh <branch> <worktree-path> on a clean merge produces a merge commit that passes all pre-commit hooks on the first attempt
  ← Satisfies: "When the orchestrator runs harvest-worktree.sh, a clean worktree merge completes in a single invocation"
- When this story is complete, harvest-worktree.sh exits 2 with a diagnostic message when the worktree's test-gate-status or review-status is missing or failed
  ← Satisfies: "When a worktree branch's test-gate-status or review-status is missing or failed, harvest-worktree.sh refuses to merge"
- When this story is complete, harvest-worktree.sh exits 1 and runs git merge --abort when the merge produces conflicts in files other than .test-index
  ← Satisfies: "When the merge produces conflicts in files other than .test-index, harvest-worktree.sh aborts the merge"
- When this story is complete, harvest-worktree.sh cleans up MERGE_HEAD via trap on any failure path, leaving the working tree in a consistent state for the next operation
  ← Satisfies: scenario mitigation for "MERGE_HEAD persists after failed harvest"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] MERGE_HEAD must not persist after failure — trap-based cleanup is critical
- [Reliability] Hash must be computed AFTER all staging (including .test-index union resolution) to prevent divergence with pre-commit hook
- [Reliability] Script should verify no MERGE_HEAD exists on entry to prevent colliding with a prior failed merge
- [Testing] Integration test should create a real worktree, commit with passing gates, then verify harvest produces a clean merge commit

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Consideration (adversarial review): [Integration] After --no-commit merge, MERGE_HEAD is present when pre-commit hooks fire. This triggers the merge-bypass path in both gates (ms_is_merge_in_progress returns true). The gates filter staged files to worktree-only (session-side) files. If all staged files are incoming-only, gates exit 0 without checking attestation. Verify --attest writes to the correct artifacts dir (session's, not worktree's) and that the gate path (merge-bypass vs full) is explicitly tested.
comments.author: Test
